issues.title: TextSelection is lost when clicking on a button
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Update the files as stated below: main.dart
3. Run `flutter run -d chrome`.
4. Write text in the TextField, select some of it, and press the button

The problem appears to be only on web, works fine in mobile.

main.dart:
```
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const Play(),
    );
  }
}

class Play extends StatefulWidget {
  const Play({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  _PlayState createState() => _PlayState();
}

class _PlayState extends State<Play> {
  final TextEditingController _controller = TextEditingController();
  final FocusNode _node = FocusNode();

  void _onPressed() {
    TextSelection selection = _controller.selection;

    print("selection.start: ${selection.start}");
    print("selection.end: ${selection.end}");
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: Column(
        mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
        children: [
          TextFormField(
            focusNode: _node,
            controller: _controller,
          ),
          ElevatedButton(
            onPressed: _onPressed,
            child: const Text("do something"),
          ),
        ],
      ),
    );
  }
}


```



**Expected results:**
The TextSelection should mirror the one actually performed.

**Actual results:**
The focus on the TextField was lost, the selection dropped and the TextSelection goes from -1 to -1 aka no selection at all.

comments:
Verified this behavior using latest master and stable on mobile, web and desktop and below is the observation:

1. Mobile:

Selecting a text and then tapping a button keeps the selection intact:

![Screenshot 2021-09-28 at 11 40 29 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67046386/135033995-c6b4cc50-3b4e-4257-858b-32af6eb2c865.png)

```
flutter: selection.start: 8
flutter: selection.end: 20
flutter: selection.start: 8
flutter: selection.end: 20
```

2. Web: (using both renderers), selecting part of a text / sentence and then tapping on a button, removes the selection:

![Screenshot 2021-09-28 at 11 37 40 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67046386/135034061-401c2421-d982-4e1d-aeb0-50bda3e93c4a.png)
![Screenshot 2021-09-28 at 11 37 48 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67046386/135034068-03668664-6aff-4503-b0e6-8c972119f567.png)

```
selection.start: -1
selection.end: -1
selection.start: -1
selection.end: -1
```
Above selection.start and selection.end shows up in stable.

On latest master though running on web, the `selection.start` and `selection.end` matches with mobile:

```
selection.start: 10
selection.end: 21
selection.start: 9
selection.end: 21
```

But user behavior is same, ie, selection is removed after tapping on a button.

3. Desktop: Same behavior as web.


Keeping this open for further insights from web team on expected / intended behavior.

This seems to be the standard behavior on the web. One thing I haven't checked is whether the selection is cleared before or after the click event (i.e. does the app get a chance to process the selection before it disappears). I'll leave this bug open to investigate.

@goderbauer what's the desired behavior on the framework side? I'm not sure if our usage of the `<input>` element is causing this, so it's hard to say if the fix is in the framework or in the web engine.
Maybe related? https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39283159/how-to-keep-selection-but-also-press-button/39285021#39285021
To clarify: It worked a few weeks ago as I developed the package [light_html_editor](https://pub.dev/packages/light_html_editor). The behavior started after the `flutter upgrade`.
I'm going to make it a framework issue until we know there's work to do on the engine side. Preserving selection on button click should be handled by the framework.
/cc @justinmc Is this related to the focus trap issue you were recently working on?

Sorry to dig this up, but in my personal experience, a very basic form with autocomplete functonality is inherintly broken and unusable.
It's even annoying to use the autocomplete. It's very unintuitive, doesn't autofill all the fields at once.

IMO this issue makes it yet another bug why you shouldn't be using Flutter to begin with. It's advertised as being super-easy to use for all platforms. It's really not.

To autofill multiple fields at once, you need to wrap them in a AutofillGroup widget. I think, that the current autofill problems can be fixed in near future and then deliver a really good user experience, but for now it's not properly working, at least on web.

issues.title: Child is drawn above a border while animating
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

[Dartpad](https://dartpad.dev/?id=1a26608234fe8f03e025c94649f45a8f)
I know it really isn't visible here ; it is however jumps to the eye on mobile. Here's a [screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5080204/172926821-0cebd5ad-2282-466c-b4dc-084a539cbb9a.png) I took where you can see two white pixels on the right side of the border, which come from the TextField. Screenshot taken as I was spamming the right-side arrow (used to open/close the widget), try it yourself but you'll need to squint your eyes.

I'm mainly here to understand if this is intended behavior from Flutter, and if so, what is the standard way to go about solving this particular issue.

**Expected results:**
The child isn't drawn above the specified border of the container.
comments:

I am experiencing it on an Android 10 device, Android 12 emulator, and Dartpad, all on the stable channel.
You probably couldn't reproduce it become it only appears during the animation. To simply matters, please replace `_widthFactor.value` by `0.5` in order to obtain this result, where the issue from my first screenshot appears :
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5080204/173026470-8b7bc441-85f7-474a-933e-c5254c71fbca.png)

I do not know that this is anti aliasing (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/14288).

It looks like the white lines are appearing over the red box decoration as they animate into view.

To note : I might also be using the Animation incorrectly. It makes sense that the child is drawn _above_ the parent. What doesn't (to me, at least) is that the border is affected by this. The average human brain expects the content not to cross the border.
But if someone has a solution to this by using Flutter in a different way I'm all for it, of course.
It may be a bug, or is may be an issue an issue of composition in your code, I am not sure. You may find a quicker answer to debugging this code by asking on stackoverflow. :)
Stackoverflow won't take too kindly to me asking "is there anything wrong with this code" I'm afraid x)

issues.title: TextField TextInputType "name" overrides TextCapitalization "words"
issues.body:
It appears that `TextField`'s `keyboardType` of `name` prevents the iOS keyboard from offering capital letters at the beginning of words, even when specifying `textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.words`.

Repro:
```
TextField(
  keyboardType: TextInputType.name,
  textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.words,
);
```

**Expected result:**
When the user selects the `TextField`, the keyboard pops up with uppercase characters.

**Actual result:**
When the user selects the `TextField`, the keyboard pops up with lowercase character.

This same issue might happen on Android but I haven't looked.

**Flutter version:**
```
Flutter 1.22.0-10.0.pre.9 • channel master • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision 8fa5c55e54 (5 weeks ago) • 2020-08-30 19:05:20 -0700

```

issues.title: Updated AppBar action button during validate callback in TextFormField
issues.body:
Internal: b/292548353

Requirement: 
When `TextFormField` validate callback detects an error, update the AppBar in the Scaffold to disable an action button. 
I was trying to call setState in the validate callback to trigger another widget.build() call. Unfortunately I get "setState() or `markNeedsBuild()` called during build." error. 

What is the correct way to do this behavior. 

I am doing a work around by attaching an onChange callback inside a Form widget that wraps the `TextFormField`. Call validate() on the `TextFormField`'s FormState and setState that way. 

Since Form is not required for `TextFormField`, this doesn't seem like the correct way of doing this.
comments: Asked another way, how do you idiomatically set a button to be enabled/disabled based on form validation? Form could probably use an onValidated callback to accomplish this.
Does `scheduleMicrotask(() => setState(() {...}))` work for you? That would move the execution of setState out of the middle of the current build call. Not sure if it's the best solution.
@robertoscaramuzzi reminder to sync up with @HansMuller on this
@robertoscaramuzzi @DaveShuckerow Please sync up and see if there's a leafy solution to this since afaicr, they did not run into this issue due to putting the entire scaffold inside their form.

@robertoscaramuzzi and I discussed this. The Leafy approach doesn't use Flutter's Form at all, which means that this issue, which depends on the internal behavior of the FormState, doesn't apply to the Leafy design.  It also means that the Leafy approach can't be quickly adopted in Mulligan.

The underlying problem is that the formfields' validator() callbacks are invoked during build, and so if they contain a setState() call, they will crash.  This is done here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/form.dart#L112

The form calls every validator it contains as part of its build process when it auto-validates.

Short-term, the resolution to this is to schedule the setState() calls for after build is completed.  One approach is to use scheduleMicrotask() to wrap all setState() calls in validators.  The other approach is the one that Mulligan has used.

On Flutter's side, I think we should consider if there is a way we can streamline the Form's logic so that it does not call validator() callbacks during build.  I imagine a common use case for our forms is to update some state based on whether or not a field is valid or if the whole form is valid.  When we make calls to validator() during build, that use case becomes difficult for users to manage.

issues.title: Control modifier does not work in CharacterActivator
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project using `flutter create shortcut_bug_test`
2. Copy the provided sample code into `lib/main.dart`
3. Run the application on Windows using the editor or using `flutter run -d windows`
4. With the application window in focus, press the following keys - 
	1. Ctrl + g (Bug)
	2. Alt + g (Works)
	3. Ctrl + Alt + g (Does not work)
	4. Shift + g (Works)
	5. Ctrl + Shift + g (Does not work)
	6. Alt + Ctrl + g (Changing order of key presses also does not work)

Note that any combination of modifiers and keys work with a `SingleActivator`.
To check this, press `Ctrl+Alt+Shift+s` which should print `Ctrl+Alt+Shift+g from SingleActivator` to the console.
To check `Ctrl+g` specifically, uncomment lines 22-24 in `lib/main.dart`. The output in that case will be `Ctrl+g from SingleActivator`.

Note that happens on Windows only. Web is fine. I don't have a MacOS or Linux machine to test the behavior on those platforms.

### Expected results

A line should be printed to console for every shortcut pressed.

The expected output is as follows:
```
Ctrl+g from CharacterActivator
Alt+g
Ctrl+Alt+g
Shift+g
Ctrl+Shift+g
Ctrl+Alt+g
```

To reproduce this expected output, run this application on the Web using `flutter run -d chrome` or similar.

### Actual results

All shortcuts involving the Control key don't work.

Output on Windows:
```
flutter: Alt+g
flutter: Shift+g
```

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false,
      home: CallbackShortcuts(
        bindings: {
          // This variant using CharacterActivator does not work
          const CharacterActivator('g', control: true): () {
            print("Ctrl+g from CharacterActivator");
          },
          // But this variant using SingleActivator does work. Uncomment this to verify
          // const SingleActivator(LogicalKeyboardKey.keyG, control: true): () {
          //   print("Ctrl+g from SingleActivator");
          // },
          // Alt as modifier alone works in CharacterActivator
          const CharacterActivator('g', alt: true): () {
            print("Alt+g");
          },
          // But combining Control with it breaks it too
          const CharacterActivator('g', alt: true, control: true): () {
            print("Ctrl+Alt+g");
          },
          // Similarly, Shift+s works exactly as expected from the CharacterActivator docs
          const CharacterActivator('G'): () {
            print("Shift+g");
          },
          // But adding Control here breaks it too
          const CharacterActivator('G', control: true): () {
            print("Ctrl+Shift+g");
          },
          // Meanwhile, all three modifiers work on a SingleActivator
          const SingleActivator(LogicalKeyboardKey.keyG,
              shift: true, control: true, alt: true): () {
            print("Ctrl+Alt+Shift+g from SingleActivator");
          },
        },
        child: const Focus(
          autofocus: true,
          child: Scaffold(
            body: Center(
              child: Text('Hello, World!'),
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

_No response_

@dkwingsmt Do you have any thoughts here?
The `fyi-text-input` label is redundant with the `team-text-input` label.
Or @gspencergoog any thoughts on why these specific key shortcut combinations aren't picked up?

Perhaps because Ctrl-G is a "Bell", which is maybe considered as a character? On old terminals back when ASCII was designed, Ctrl-G would generate a beep on the terminal if you echoed it.  So I guess I'm saying, maybe Ctrl-G is considered to generate a "character", similar to Ctrl-M and Ctrl-J.  Does it work if you use this?:

```dart
const CharacterActivator('\0x7', control: true)
```

BTW, these all work as expected on the web (at least on Linux Chrome).

> Perhaps because Ctrl-G is a "Bell", which is maybe considered as a character? On old terminals back when ASCII was designed, Ctrl-G would generate a beep on the terminal if you echoed it. So I guess I'm saying, maybe Ctrl-G is considered to generate a "character", similar to Ctrl-M and Ctrl-J.

This happens with all keys that I tested, including alphabet and number keys.
I had originally encountered this issue when attempting to add a `Ctrl+S` shortcut to my app. The reproduction was tested and submitted with `Ctrl+G` instead of `Ctrl+S` to avoid precisely this situation.

>Does it work if you use this?:
>
> ```dart
> const CharacterActivator('\0x7', control: true)
> ```

Unless I'm doing something wrong, copy-pasting this into the appropriate spot does not seem to work. It additionally stops working on the web too with this change.

As an aside, the linter seems to be flagging this as `dart(unnecessary_string_escapes)` so maybe this example is not behaving as you'd expect it to.

> BTW, these all work as expected on the web (at least on Linux Chrome).

The original issue was for Windows only, with web being used to show the expected behaviour.

> This happens with all keys that I tested, including alphabet and number keys. Okay, I guess that rules out my guess. As additional data: I tried this on Linux desktop, and it works as expected on main channel.

issues.title: [iOS][PlatformView] Unable to type in UITextView with physical keyboard
issues.body:
There is a fatal [bug](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/70267) in Flutter's Japanese text input.
Therefore, in my app, UITextView is displayed as PlatformView.
After supporting Flutter2, I couldn't input with the physical keyboard.

issues.title: iOS copy/paste bar is available by 3-tap gesture even after TextField has been destroyed
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

From my understanding this was introduced sometime between 3.7.9 and 3.10.x

1. Launch app in iOS
2. Use three fingers to tap at the same time (nothing happens)
3. Go to screen with a CupertinoTextField
4. Interact with the TextField
5. Use three fingers to tap at the same time (cut/copy/paste iOS menu appears at top of screen)
6. Change to a different view so that the view with the CupertinoTextField is destroyed
7. Use three fingers to tap at the same time (cut/copy/paste iOS menu appears at top of screen)

### Expected results

I would not expect the 3-tap gesture to show the iOS menu in step 7. 

### Actual results

The 3-tap gesture shows the iOS menu even when there is no TextField present.

### Code sample

    ...

),

issues.title: Textspan's tapGestureRecognizer has unexpected behaviour when the textspan is out of the textfield visible area after the first layout
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample (see "Code sample" section below) 
2. Like the code shows below, I want to insert custom string (like "[ hello ]" "[ world ]" ) or common text into a textfiled by override "buildTextSpan" in TextEditingController. 
3. And I add recognizer to each textSpan. But I find  that the textspans which displayed with not exceeding the bounds of textfiled can get a right onTap response, showing the right callback number. The textspans which displayed with exceeding the bounds of textfiled can not get a right callback number.
4. A video is provided below.


**Expected results:** Like the video shows,  when I click the number, the output should be the number clicked.

**Actual results:** when I click 0~44 textspan , it works like a charm. But when I click other number, it prints the number at the range between 0 and 44.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```
import 'package:flutter/gestures.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(
      title: 'Retrieve Text Input',
      home: MyCustomForm(),
    );
  }
}

class MyCustomForm extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyCustomForm({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  State<MyCustomForm> createState() => _MyCustomFormState();
}

// Define a corresponding State class.
// This class holds data related to the Form.
class _MyCustomFormState extends State<MyCustomForm> {
  // Create a text controller and use it to retrieve the current value
  // of the TextField.
  String? _clickText;
  late final RichTextEditingController myController;

  @override
  void initState() {
    super.initState();
    myController = RichTextEditingController((text) {
      setState(() {
        _clickText = text;
      });
    });
    // Start listening to changes.
    myController.addListener(_printLatestValue);
  }

  @override
  void dispose() {
    // Clean up the controller when the widget is removed from the widget tree.
    // This also removes the _printLatestValue listener.
    myController.dispose();
    super.dispose();
  }

  void _printLatestValue() {
    print('Second text field: ${myController.text}');
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(
          title: const Text('Retrieve Text Input'),
        ),
        body: Column(
          children: [
            TextField(
              scrollPadding: const EdgeInsets.all(20.0),
              minLines: 1,
              maxLines: 3,
              maxLength: 100,
              maxLengthEnforcement:
                  MaxLengthEnforcement.truncateAfterCompositionEnds,
              onChanged: (text) {},
              controller: myController,
              decoration: InputDecoration(
                  fillColor: Colors.grey[50],
                  filled: true,
                  contentPadding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(
                      horizontal: 8, vertical: 10)), 
            ),
            Text(_clickText ?? ''),
          ],
        ));
  }
}

class RichTextEditingController extends TextEditingController {
  final Function(String) changeTextCallback;

  RichTextEditingController(this.changeTextCallback);
  @override
  TextSpan buildTextSpan(
      {required BuildContext context,
      TextStyle? style,
      required bool withComposing}) {
    if (!value.isComposingRangeValid || !withComposing) {
      final List<InlineSpan> inlineSpans =
          buildInlineSpans(TextRange(start: 0, end: text.length), style);
      return TextSpan(
        children: inlineSpans,
      );
    }
    final TextStyle composingStyle =
        style?.merge(const TextStyle(decoration: TextDecoration.underline)) ??
            const TextStyle(decoration: TextDecoration.underline);
    return TextSpan(
      children: <TextSpan>[
        TextSpan(
            children: buildInlineSpans(
                TextRange(start: 0, end: value.composing.start), style)),
        TextSpan(
            children: buildInlineSpans(
                TextRange(
                    start: value.composing.start, end: value.composing.end),
                composingStyle)),
        TextSpan(
            children: buildInlineSpans(
                TextRange(start: value.composing.end, end: text.length),
                style)),
      ],
    );
  }

  List<InlineSpan> buildInlineSpans(TextRange range, TextStyle? style) {
    List<TextSpan> list = [];
    for (var i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
      final pp = TextSpan(
          text: '[ ' '$i ' ' ]',
          recognizer: TapGestureRecognizer()
            ..onTap = () {
              changeTextCallback(i.toString());
            });
      list.add(pp);
    }
    return [
      WidgetSpan(
        alignment: PlaceholderAlignment.baseline,
        baseline: TextBaseline.alphabetic,
        child: Text.rich(
          TextSpan(children: list),
          style: const TextStyle(fontSize: 13),
        ),
      )
    ];
  }
}

> The 2nd example is similar to an existing issue #124205.

Flutter has some issues when it comes to keyboard and how it behaves across iOS, Android, iOS web and Android web.

Scaffold and SingleChildScrollView works for mobile apps but for mobile web browsers, you have to tweak it.

Secret is to set resizeToAvoidBottomInset to false on Android web on your scaffold.

Also make sure that if you have nested scaffolds then same setting should be applied there as well.

For iOS web, there is another problem where the textfield doesn't loose focus when you tap the "Done" button on the keyboard accessory view and to handle that you have to apply iOS web related checks. Check out this detailed post with full code sample: https://cleancodestack.com/flutter-keyboard-issues-fix-ios-android-and-web/

@huycozy 

## Context
- Scaffold.resizeToAvoidBottomInset is not set (default value)
- flutter version 3.24.5
- android, pixel 8, api 34

## Steps to reproduce
1. Open a bottom sheet and focus on a text field (to trigger the keyboard).
2. Dismiss the bottom sheet.

## Behaviour
Actual: visually the FAB jumps down when bottom sheet is totally hidden.
Expected: the FAB should remain on the bottom Throughout the bottom sheet flow.

## Question
Is this issue related to this ticket? Or maybe even to your Case 1?

/cc @gspencergoog
This is as designed, since `onTapOutside` is ignored if the text field doesn't have focus, and once you tap down outside, it no longer has focus by the time the tap up occurs.
This was introduced in this PR: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/136291

You're probably right that `TapRegion` is poorly named: it doesn't process the pointer events as gestures, only as individual events. This is because one design goal with it was to make sure that it didn't didn't introduce delays and side effects into the processing of pointer events, so that a pointer down "outside" could still be recognized by a separate gesture detector as a tap or other gesture. Consequently, the `TapRegion` doesn't participate in the gesture arena at all.

The right widget for detecting gestures is the `GestureDetector`, of course, but I'm not sure it will solve your problem, since you want to detect gestures anywhere except the widget that has focus.

Would it help to have a callback on the `TapRegion` that is called only when there is a pointer up event outside? Or maybe we can make the behavior of the `TapRegion` with respect to focus be toggleable so that it can send a callback even if it doesn't have focus? (or maybe both?)

> The right widget for detecting gestures is the GestureDetector, of course, but I'm not sure it will solve your problem, since you want to detect gestures anywhere except the widget that has focus.

I thought `TapRegion` and "tap outside" was specifically designed to solve the problem that I've described? In fact, I thought it was previously working as I've described? There were requests for years to be able to close the keyboard when tapping outside a text field. There were various hacks to do that. But then I thought `TapRegion` was introduced, in part, to solve that long requested feature?

> Would it help to have a callback on the TapRegion that is called only when there is a pointer up event outside? Or maybe we can make the behavior of the TapRegion with respect to focus be toggleable so that it can send a callback even if it doesn't have focus? (or maybe both?)

I'm confused about what focus has to do with any of this. Can you elaborate on that? If `TapRegion` reports a tap down, why would reporting a tap up require some kind of different focus state? `TapRegion` doesn't change focus automatically does it? I thought the developer needed to change focus within the `onTapOutside` callback?

As for your specific question, yes, if we receive both the tap down and tap up, then we can differentiate between a tap and a drag, and then we can choose to only `unfocus` on a tap, so the user can drag with the keyboard open.
@gspencergoog do you have any input on my use-case description above?

> I thought TapRegion and "tap outside" was specifically designed to solve the problem that I've described? In fact, I thought it was previously working as I've described? There were requests for years to be able to close the keyboard when tapping outside a text field. There were various hacks to do that. But then I thought TapRegion was introduced, in part, to solve that long requested feature?

Yes, this is why `GestureDetector` won't solve your problem.

> I'm confused about what focus has to do with any of this. Can you elaborate on that? If TapRegion reports a tap down, why would reporting a tap up require some kind of different focus state? TapRegion doesn't change focus automatically does it? I thought the developer needed to change focus within the onTapOutside callback?

Focus has to do with this because of the specific use case for text fields where not taking focus into consideration caused this issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/136291

I think one solution here might just be to make the focus-sensitivity of `TextFieldTapRegion` be settable, and false by default, and turn it on only for `TextField`s.

Another would be to revert https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/136291 and tell people to check the focus state they care about inside of the `onTapOutside`. We'd then move the focus check into the [default `onTapOutside`](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/45a4c7a4327046bbaa7d3800fae6af572b0c69da/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L4704) behavior instead of checking the focus before we call `onTapOutside`.  That way, the usual case of not specifying an `onTapOutside` will still only activate if the field has focus, but you can specify an `onTapOutside` to override that behavior.

> Another would be to revert https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/136291 and tell people to check the focus state they care about inside of the onTapOutside.

This one makes the most sense to me. It should be trivial to check the focus of the thing you care about. And then you leave all the doors open for any other behavior. I would generally try to avoid imposing a behavior on a general-purpose widget for a single use-case. That's what composition is for. Moreover, as we can see by this ticket, the focus requirement actually impedes a category of text field use-cases, so it didn't even really solve that issue, either.

> Consequently, the `TapRegion` doesn't participate in the gesture arena at all.

I think we should have a `TapRegion` equivalent that participates in gesture arena. I have a similar problem, but having access to `tapUp` or reverting #136291 won't help me (although I agree with @matthew-carroll that it **should** be reverted).

In my case, I am using the `onTapOutside` to unfocus the focused widget. However, if a user has the keyboard open an try to close it using the back gesture (right to left drag), the first "tap down" of the gesture will make the text field unfocus, and the gesture will make the app navigate back.

Related issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/140271

Correct me if I'm wrong, but I don't think reverting #136291 would be enough, since `TapRegion`only triggers the callback on `PointerDownEvent`.

I suggest adding `onTapUpOutside` and `onTapUpInside`, and possibly deprecating `onTapOutside` and `onTapInside` in favor on new `onTapDownOutside` and `onTapDownInside` callbacks.

I think that would solve your issue as well @mateusfccp? If you don't unfocus on tap down?
This should be fixed by #156110. Can someone confirm?

> I think that would solve your issue as well @mateusfccp? If you don't unfocus on tap down?

issues.title: [SelectableText] Edit menu position is incorrect when Selected text exceeds the Screen size

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Use `SelectableText(LONG_TEXT, showCursor: true)` **Edit:** Text needs to be longer than fits on screen
2. Highlight text -> Select All
3. Text is selected but `Copy` is not an option. Tap/LongTap will remove the selection.

If you scroll up, you should see the context menu at the very top of the selected text. The behavior of 'Select All' will scroll you to the bottom of whatever text you're selecting, and the menu is scrolled out of view.

Can the Copy/Paste menu be snapped to the top/bottom of the visible text in this scenario?

Edit: On `master` I'm seeing the menu appear below the text selection now (solves my issue). There may have been a recent change the invalidates my previous observation.

Edit 2: It does however get obscured behind the software keyboard if scrolled.

I am able to reproduce the issue on the latest stable and master channel on both IOS and Android. It looks like the edit menu is incorrectly positioned hence the issue.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/203137526-5449c498-52af-4a0b-95c2-5e0ca9071629.mov


Setting ` physics: const BouncingScrollPhysics(),` in the above [code sample](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/59707#issuecomment-647332851) will show the same behavior on Android.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/203138111-6aac8795-a2ff-4d00-afd4-2430a53c0212.mov


It looks like a duplicate of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/56176

issues.title: the selection highlight should expand for the full width of the input.
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce 1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample 2. Tap `TextField` and Tap `Select All` **Expected results:** the selection highlight expands for the full width of the input like native app. **Actual results:** it shrinks.
Code sample ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() => runApp(const MyApp()); class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { final TextEditingController controller = TextEditingController(text: 'abc\ndef\nghi'); return MaterialApp( home: Directionality( textDirection: TextDirection.ltr, child: MediaQuery( data: const MediaQueryData(size: Size(800.0, 600.0)), child: Align( alignment: Alignment.bottomCenter, child: Material( child: Center( child: TextField( decoration: const InputDecoration(isCollapsed: true), style: const TextStyle(fontSize: 32, height: 1), maxLines: 3, controller: controller, ), ), ), ), ), ), ); } } ```

issues.title: [Windows][HardwareKeyboard] Incorrect Caps Lock status when starting the app with Caps Lock On
comments:
@nosmirck 
Thanks for the report. I verified this behavior on latest stable and master on Windows (desktop + web) and observed that tapping `caps lock` does change the status from OFF to ON and vice-versa, but as soon as we release the key, the status changes, ie, the status doesn't remain the same upon key release, as shown below:


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67046386/153858101-ffd4f51b-4e2c-45e4-bdb7-2c0ce66929ea.MOV

On macOS though, it works as expected, ie, tapping `caps lock` keeps the status as expected upon key press and key release and we have to tap the key again to change the status.

Is this a correct interpretation of the issue ? and you are expecting that the status to remain persistent upon key release as well ?

@darshankawar yes, that's exactly what I encountered.

There's a discrepancy in the behaviour for caps lock.

It has to behave either as it does on Mac or as it does on Windows, however either way has its own issues.

In my opinion, it should remain as it does on Windows (keysPressed contains the keys that are actually pressed down) so we are able to detect when caps lock is down or not, however, we need a special case for caps lock, num lock and scroll lock. These are keys that have toggle states beyond just being pressed or not. 

Natively, the state that is reported by the keyboard (on Windows) is given by [this function](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-ca/windows/win32/api/winuser/nf-winuser-getkeystate?redirectedfrom=MSDN) that has this in the documentation:

> Retrieves the status of the specified virtual key. The status specifies whether the key is up, down, or toggled (on, off—alternating each time the key is pressed).

So, for each key the status is represented with a short (8bits) number where the highest bit represent the pressed status (1 is pressed, 0 is not) and the lowest bit represents the toggle state (if any). So, any of the lock keys will have a toggle status but the rest of the keys won't.

I think flutter should implement something similar, it should report the toggle status separate from the pressed status.

Thanks for the details. Based on my findings and above feedback,  the functioning of pressing caps lock button to change know the status is different on Windows and on macOS.

On Windows, using provided code sample, the caps lock status does change, but it goes away as soon as we release the key press event.
On MacOS, the caps lock status remains as expected, ie, pressing key press and key release works properly.

It's an known oversight of the `RawKeyboard` API, and the macOS behavior is actually, to some extent, considered a bug (due to how macOS sends this flag). Unfortunately we do not plan to change this behavior of `RawKeyboard`.

But, the `HardwareKeyboard` API, which will be the standard API in the future, does provide such feature at [HardwareKeyboard.lockModesEnabled](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/HardwareKeyboard/lockModesEnabled.html) . Let me know if this fulfills your need.
